verdict.summary: SSL certificate cannot be loaded. File not found, wrong permissions, invalid PEM format, or incomplete certificate chain.

dead_ends.action: Generate a self-signed certificate for production
dead_ends.why_fails: Self-signed certificates trigger browser warnings and are rejected by most clients. Use Let's Encrypt for free valid certificates.

dead_ends.action: Set ssl_verify_client off assuming it fixes the error
dead_ends.why_fails: ssl_verify_client controls client certificate verification, not server certificate loading. Different directive entirely.

workarounds.action: Check file permissions and ownership on certificate files
workarounds.how: ls -la /etc/nginx/ssl/; chown root:root cert.pem key.pem; chmod 644 cert.pem; chmod 600 key.pem

workarounds.action: Verify the certificate chain is complete and in correct order
workarounds.how: openssl verify -CAfile ca-bundle.crt cert.pem; cat cert.pem intermediate.pem > fullchain.pem  # server cert first, then intermediates

workarounds.action: Validate PEM format and key match
workarounds.how: openssl x509 -noout -modulus -in cert.pem | md5sum; openssl rsa -noout -modulus -in key.pem | md5sum  # must match
